description: Working at the intersection of AI, automation, and elite sports technology at the market-leading sports data company, contributing to production-grade QA engineering and AI systems.
bullets: Building production-grade solutions combining Python, AI tooling, and test automation for sports data pipelines and analytics systems used by professional leagues and broadcasters worldwide
Leveraging LLMs and AI copilots as daily development force-multipliers — translating vague specifications into precise, testable requirements and reliable automated validation systems
Participating in AI-driven prototyping and rapid iteration at the intersection of specification-driven engineering, agentic AI design patterns, and real-world sports data simulation

role: App Developer Intern — Backend System & Microservices
description: Architected and delivered a production-ready backend from scratch, owning the full stack from API design to cloud deployment.
bullets: Architected and delivered a production-ready backend using Node.js and Express, building 20+ REST API endpoints with scalable microservices architecture, API Gateway pattern, and authored technical API documentation reviewed via GitHub PRs
Implemented JWT auth (access/refresh tokens), HTTP-only cookies, Google & X OAuth, idempotency keys, and rate limiting — enforcing stateless, token-scoped session control

Containerized 3+ fault-tolerant backend services using Docker and Docker Compose, deployed on AWS EC2 with automated CI/CD pipelines via GitHub Actions — cutting manual deployment effort by ~70% and achieving API response times under 150ms in production
